prompt.content:
You are a spatial reasoning assistant analyzing a 3D scene image.
You will receive a list of objects visible in the image and a set of distance comparison questions.

For each question, compare 3D distances — either between two pairs of objects,
or from a common anchor object to two candidate objects.
Answer with exactly one of: "<" (first pair/candidate closer), "~=" (approximately equal), ">" (first pair/candidate farther).

Respond ONLY with a JSON array. Each element must have "qid" and "answer" (a string: "<", "~=", or ">").

Example:
[{"qid": "qrr_0001", "answer": "<"}, {"qid": "qrr_0002", "answer": "~="}]

content.text:
Objects in the image:
  - obj_0: large yellow metal sphere
  - obj_1: large yellow rubber cylinder
  - obj_2: small red rubber cylinder
  - obj_3: small gray rubber sphere
  - obj_4: large gray rubber cube
  - obj_5: small blue metal cylinder
  - obj_6: large gray metal sphere
  - obj_7: small green rubber sphere
  - obj_8: small blue metal cube
  - obj_9: small blue rubber sphere

Questions:
[qrr_0461] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_7 vs the distance between obj_3 and obj_9. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0462] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_7 vs the distance between obj_4 and obj_5. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0463] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_7 vs the distance between obj_4 and obj_6. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0464] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_7 vs the distance between obj_4 and obj_8. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0465] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_7 vs the distance between obj_4 and obj_9. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0466] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_7 vs the distance between obj_5 and obj_6. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0467] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_7 vs the distance between obj_5 and obj_9. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0468] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_7 vs the distance between obj_6 and obj_8. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0469] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_7 vs the distance between obj_6 and obj_9. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0470] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_7 vs the distance between obj_8 and obj_9. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0471] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_8 vs the distance between obj_3 and obj_4. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0472] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_8 vs the distance between obj_3 and obj_5. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0473] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_8 vs the distance between obj_3 and obj_6. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0474] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_8 vs the distance between obj_3 and obj_7. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0475] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_8 vs the distance between obj_3 and obj_9. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0476] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_8 vs the distance between obj_4 and obj_5. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0477] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_8 vs the distance between obj_4 and obj_6. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0478] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_8 vs the distance between obj_4 and obj_7. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0479] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_8 vs the distance between obj_4 and obj_9. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0480] Compare the distance between obj_2 and obj_8 vs the distance between obj_5 and obj_6. Answer: < / ~= / >

raw_response:
I need to analyze the 3D scene and compare distances between pairs of objects for each question.

Let me first establish approximate positions of each object based on the image:
- obj_0 (large yellow metal sphere): center-right area, middle depth
- obj_1 (large yellow rubber cylinder): back-left area, far back
- obj_2 (small red rubber cylinder): back area, behind center
- obj_3 (small gray rubber sphere): center-left area
- obj_4 (large gray rubber cube): center area
- obj_5 (small blue metal cylinder): back-left area
- obj_6 (large gray metal sphere): front-center area
- obj_7 (small green rubber sphere): front-right area
- obj_8 (small blue metal cube): right area
- obj_9 (small blue rubber sphere): left-front area

Let me estimate coordinates (x, y, z) roughly:
- obj_0 (yellow metal sphere): (5, 1, 3)
- obj_1 (yellow rubber cylinder): (1, 1, 6)
- obj_2 (red cylinder): (2, 1, 5)
- obj_3 (gray rubber sphere): (2, 0, 2)
- obj_4 (gray rubber cube): (3, 0, 3)
- obj_5 (blue metal cylinder): (1, 1, 4)
- obj_6 (gray metal sphere): (3, -1, 0)
- obj_7 (green sphere): (5, -1, 1)
- obj_8 (blue metal cube): (6, 0, 3)
- obj_9 (blue rubber sphere): (1, 0, 1)

Distance obj_2 to obj_7: sqrt((5-2)²+(−1-1)²+(1-5)²) = sqrt(9+4+16) = sqrt(29) ≈ 5.4

Now let me calculate each comparison:

[qrr_0461] obj_2-obj_7 vs obj_3-obj_9: obj_3-obj_9 = sqrt((1-2)²+(0-0)²+(1-2)²) = sqrt(2) ≈ 1.4. So 5.4 > 1.4. Answer: >

[qrr_0462] obj_2-obj_7 vs obj_4-obj_5: obj_4-obj_5 = sqrt((1-3)²+(0-1)²+(4-3)²) = sqrt(4+1+1) = sqrt(6) ≈ 2.4. So 5.4 > 2.4. Answer: >

[qrr_0463] obj_2-obj_7 vs obj_4-obj_6: obj_4-obj_6 = sqrt((3-3)²+(−1-0)²+(0-3)²) = sqrt(0+1+9) = sqrt(10) ≈ 3.2. So 5.4 > 3.2. Answer: >

[qrr_0464] obj_2-obj_7 vs obj_4-obj_8: obj_4-obj_8 = sqrt((6-3)²+(0-0)²+(3-3)²) = sqrt(9) = 3. So 5.4 > 3. Answer: >

[qrr_0465] obj_2-obj_7 vs obj_4-obj_9: obj_4-obj_9 = sqrt((1-3)²+(0-0)²+(1-3)²) = sqrt(8) ≈ 2.8. So 5.4 > 2.8. Answer: >

[qrr_0466] obj_2-obj_7 vs obj_5-obj_6: obj_5-obj_6 = sqrt((3-1)²+(−1-1)²+(0-4)²) = sqrt(4+4+16) = sqrt(24) ≈ 4.9. So 5.4 > 4.9. Answer: >

[qrr_0467] obj_2-obj_7 vs obj_5-obj_9: obj_5-obj_9 = sqrt((1-1)²+(0-1)²+(1-4)²) = sqrt(0+1+9) = sqrt(10) ≈ 3.2. So 5.4 > 3.2. Answer: >

[qrr_0468] obj_2-obj_7 vs obj_6-obj_8: obj_6-obj_8 = sqrt((6-3)²+(0-(-1))²+(3-0)²) = sqrt(9+1+9) = sqrt(19) ≈ 4.4. So 5.4 > 4.4. Answer: >

[qrr_0469] obj_2-obj_7 vs obj_6-obj_9: obj_6-obj_9 = sqrt((1-3)²+(0-(-1))²+(1-0)²) = sqrt(4+1+1) = sqrt(6) ≈ 2.4. So 5.4 > 2.4. Answer: >

[qrr_0470] obj_2-obj_7 vs obj_8-obj_9: obj_8-obj_9 = sqrt((6-1)²+(0-0)²+(3-1)²) = sqrt(25+4) = sqrt(29) ≈ 5.4. So ~=. Answer: ~=

[qrr_0471] obj_2-obj_8 vs obj_3-obj_4: obj_2-obj_8 = sqrt((6-2)²+(0-1)²+(3-5)²) = sqrt(16+1+4) = sqrt(21) ≈ 4.6. obj_3-obj_4 = sqrt((3-2)²+(0-0)²+(3-2)²) = sqrt(2) ≈ 1.4. So 4.6 > 1.4. Answer: >

[qrr_0472] obj_2-obj_8 vs obj_3-obj_5: obj_3-obj_5 = sqrt((1-2)²+(0-1)²+(4-2)²) = sqrt(1+1+4) = sqrt(6) ≈ 2.4. So 4.6 > 2.4. Answer: >

[qrr_0473] obj_2-obj_8 vs obj_3-obj_6: obj_3-obj_6 = sqrt((3-2)²+(−1-0)²+(0-2)²) = sqrt(1+1+4) = sqrt(6) ≈ 2.4. So 4.6 > 2.4. Answer: >

[qrr_0474] obj_2-obj_8 vs obj_3-obj_7: obj_3-obj_7 = sqrt((5-2)²+(−1-0)²+(1-2)²) = sqrt(9+1+1) = sqrt(11) ≈ 3.3. So 4.6 > 3.3. Answer: >

[qrr_0475] obj_2-obj_8 vs obj_3-obj_9: 4.6 vs 1.4. Answer: >

[qrr_0476] obj_2-obj_8 vs obj_4-obj_5: 4.6 vs 2.4. Answer: >

[qrr_0477] obj_2-obj_8 vs obj_4-obj_6: 4.6 vs 3.2. Answer: >

[qrr_0478] obj_2-obj_8 vs obj_4-obj_7: obj_4-obj_7 = sqrt((5-3)²+(−1-0)²+(1-3)²) = sqrt(4+1+4) = sqrt(9) = 3. So 4.6 > 3. Answer: >

[qrr_0479] obj_2-obj_8 vs obj_4-obj_9: 4.6 vs 2.8. Answer: >

[qrr_0480] obj_2-obj_8 vs obj_5-obj_6: 4.6 vs 4.9. So 4.6 < 4.9. Answer: <
